title: Swipr
html:
Swipr

Swipe-based prediction app — TikTok mechanics + Tinder preference filters + Polymarket/Kalshi-style markets. The most legally-fraught of the stealth-apps cluster. Direct lineal descendant of WagerWrld (Jan 2024), sibling to Vrtl Bet, cousin to Outpace.

TL;DR

  • Core mechanic: session-based accuracy betting — users complete batches of 10-50 yes/no predictions and earn tiered payouts on overall hit rate (60% → 1.2x, 70% → 2x, 80% → 5x). Skill-not-chance framing.
  • Engineered house edge: mix gimme + coin-flip + long-shot predictions per session — users feel they're at 50/50, the math says otherwise.
  • Business-model trick: charge session entry fees instead of per-bet rake. Lower infra cost (no real-time data streams), higher viral surface (share upcoming sessions with friends).
  • Closest competitor: A16Z-backed Cheddar (cheddr.xyz) (sweepstakes loophole, targets 18-21yo). Past failures (Bookee 2016, Betr) tied themselves to live-sports infra; Yahir's edge is using events that resolve over hours/days.
  • Payment rail: Aeropay. Dominant cost: legal — "can't fuck this up."

Lineage (the peer-wager-app thread)

Date Doc Form
2024-01-16 WagerWrld memo 1v1 parlay duels, Venmo-replacement, crypto-as-regulatory-arbitrage
2024-07-04 mega junk drawer "App to bet within friend — simple odds creation, group bets where others impact odds"
2024-12-28 ideas dump "TikTok Style, swipe left or right, betting app with micro prediction where payout is based on accuracy of prediction" — Swipr origin line
2025-10-20 Vrtl Bet placeholder empty doc — sibling brand stamp
2026-02-09 Swipr PRD full session-accuracy spec, competitor map, stack questions

Open questions

  • React Native vs Swift?
  • Real legal cost breakdown by US state?
  • Has Swipr advanced past concept doc? (the "dfdfd" placeholder mid-PRD suggests early-draft)
  • Cross-addiction thesis (sports bettor + crypto degen + political junkie + casino gambler in one feed) — realistic given divergent regulatory regimes?
